vulnerability.description: WordPress Backup Migration plugin <= 1.3.7 allows unauthenticated RCE via attacker-controlled Content-Dir HTTP header passed to require_once in includes/backup-heart.php.
vulnerability.root_cause: backup-heart.php builds BMI_INCLUDES from the Content-Dir request header without validation, then require_once on that path.
vulnerability.attack_scenario: Step 1: Confirm plugin at /wp-content/plugins/backup-backup/readme.txt (stable tag <= 1.3.7). Step 2: POST to /wp-content/plugins/backup-backup/includes/backup-heart.php with HTTP header Content-Dir set to a SHORT php://filter chain (not a POST body parameter named content). Step 3: Stage fwrite payloads character-by-character — full filter chains exceed Apache header limits. Step 4: GET the dropped webshell under wp-content/plugins/backup-backup/includes/*.php?0=cat%20/opt/flag.txt. Do NOT use POST body content= — that is wrong for this CVE.

primitives.name: content_dir_header_include
primitives.description: Content-Dir header controls path passed to require_once

parameters.name: Content-Dir
parameters.description: php://filter chain payload; must stay under server header size limit
parameters.encoding_notes: Use staged short chains like <?php fwrite(fopen('x','w'),'a');?> — not a POST form field

success_signals.description: CTF flag from /opt/flag.txt via dropped webshell

avoid: single long filter chain in one header
exploit_hints.notes: Bundled module kavach.exploit.modules.wp_backup_migration handles staged exploit when LLM HTTP attempts fail.
